text:
User: will using the archeologist generate the wiki properly? can you test with one of my projects (like this one) and review the outcome? if you know that its not going to generate properly then fix it first and then run the archeologist on this project and REVIEW the results. If adjustments are needed iterate the flow, tools or prompts until we get seriously good results. (do this in agents so you don't pollute your context window and can efficiently check)

[Perhaps it would be good to first dedicate a sonnet agent to retrieve ALL transcripts that the archeologist would process, process them via an opus agent (so basically building an opus-generated/manually-constructed wiki) and then use that as a comparison to see what the archeologist (or iterations_) would generate

Assistant: This is a meaty multi-agent task and I'll orchestrate it so my context stays clean. But first, one decision-critical check I should do myself (small): **does the archeologist even work with the v0.5 staged pipeline I just merged?** The archeologist calls into capture, and I just rewrote capture's core â€” if that integration is broken, there's no point building a gold standard or doing a 35-min run. Quick orientation:

Assistant: Key finding from orientation: the archeologist **routes through the exact v0.5 path my e2e tests already passed on** â€” `run_capture_for_archeologist` (capture.rs:2375) â†’ `run_capture_from_input` â†’ `run_wiki_agent` (the staged pipeline). So it's very likely to work. But it sets archeologist-specific flags my tests didn't cover (`skip_structural_maintenance`, `today_override` from transcript timestamps, checkpoint-time `run_structural_maintenance`), so a smoke test is warranted before a 47-session / 44MB run. That's the right opening move. [...] User: [Agent task result: Agent "Smoke + run archeologist to temp wiki" completed]
# Archeologist + v0.5 Validation Report

## Full wiki output path (durable, NOT deleted)
`/tmp/arch-full/projects/Users_pablofernandez_src_proactive-context/docs/wiki/` â€” 33 guides + `_index.md`. Backup of the real wiki (pre-run, 151 guides) preserved at `/tmp/wiki-backup.11801/`.

## Smoke result + the fix I made
**Found and fixed a real safety bug before any real run.** The orchestrator's "verified fact" â€” *`--output-dir` redirects ALL wiki writes* â€” was **FALSE in the merged code**. `wiki_path` was unconditionally `wiki_dir(project_root)` = the real repo's `docs/wiki/`. Only `marker_dir` (captured-sessions) and `proj_dir` (index.db) honored `output_dir`. A bulk run with `--output-dir` would have written/overwritten guides in the REAL 151-guide wiki, and the checkpoint structural-maintenance would have mutated it too. Two write-path callsites: `run_capture_from_input` (capture.rs:2018) and `run_checkpoint` (archeologist.rs:662).

**Fix (committed on master, `d8e610f`):** redirect `wiki_path` to `<output_dir>/projects/<normalized>/docs/wiki` when `output_dir` is set, in both callsites.

**Smoke (8 sessions, glm):** PASSED. Produced `in-argument-delay-resolution.md` in the OUTPUT dir with full v0.5 markers (`EXTRACTâ†’AUTHORITY GATEâ†’ROUTEâ†’RECONCILE`, `op create`/`op add`), proper citations, and `created:2026-05-29` from the session's historical date (today_override works). The final checkpoint `rebuilt _index.md` on the output dir. Real wiki mtime stayed `1780151529` â€” untouched.

**CLI note:** `--project` and `--yes` are mutually exclusive; the leading-dash key must be passed as the real cwd path with `=`: `--project=/Users/pablofernandez/src/proactive-context`. The encoded `-Users-...` key does NOT match (it fell through to a 2-session phantom project).

## Full-run stats (real command output, exit 0)
